concepts.name: Sensemaking
concepts.description: How humans construct meaning from ambiguous, complex information; core lens for Barbara's work.

concepts.name: Bayesian reasoning
concepts.description: Probabilistic updating of beliefs given new evidence; shapes how Barbara approaches uncertainty.

concepts.name: David Marr's levels of analysis
concepts.description: Computational / algorithmic / implementation distinction; Barbara's framework for system design.

concepts.name: Mental Models
concepts.description: Internal representations users and experts build of systems; central to human-centered design.

concepts.name: Human-centered design
concepts.description: Prioritizing user needs, comprehension, and adoption over technical elegance.

concepts.name: Systems thinking
concepts.description: Understanding complex interdependencies rather than isolated components.

concepts.name: Meaning-making
concepts.description: Transforming messy, raw data into usable insight — Barbara's core value proposition.

concepts.name: Explainability
concepts.description: Making AI/ML decisions transparent, inspectable, and trustable.

concepts.name: Representations and Constraints Framework
concepts.description: Barbara's dissertation framework: perception shaped by representational constraints on internal models.

concepts.name: Translational Research
concepts.description: Bridging academic research methods and real-world operational practice.
alt_labels: cognitive science applied to AI systems

concepts.name: Grounded AI Systems
concepts.description: AI that is operationally ready, verifiable, and trustworthy — not just technically functional.

concepts.name: Evaluation as a design problem
concepts.description: Treating evaluation as a first-class design concern, not an afterthought.

concepts.name: Pragmatic design
concepts.description: The gap between technically correct and actually useful; design for adoption, not demonstration.
alt_labels: Gap between technically correct and actually useful

concepts.name: Knowledge representation
concepts.description: How knowledge is structured, organized, and made navigable for humans and machines.

concepts.name: Transfer of domain knowledge
concepts.description: Applying cognitive science and academic research frameworks to practical AI/data problems.

concepts.name: Ontology
concepts.description: Formal classification and organization of concepts within a domain.

concepts.name: Semantic Web
concepts.description: Linked data paradigm for making knowledge machine-readable and interoperable.

concepts.name: Contextual guidance
concepts.description: Scene context and prior knowledge guide where attention goes; from Barbara's dissertation research.

concepts.name: Attention as a limited resource
concepts.description: Cognitive resource allocation under constraints; influences how Barbara thinks about information overload.

concepts.name: Design for the downstream mental model
concepts.description: Shape what users will build in their heads, not just what the system outputs.

concepts.name: Durable knowledge systems
concepts.description: Systems that hold up over time, remain inspectable, and accumulate value rather than decay.

concepts.name: Hypothesis-driven design
concepts.description: Starting from a testable claim about the world before building; experimental rigor applied to product.

projects.name: Comparative Map Analysis
projects.context: I developed a new analytical method called Comparative Map Analysis. This technique lets you compare spatial patterns in eye movement data across different observers and different viewing sessions.
